text.NRSV: How lonely sits the city that once was full of people! How like a widow she has become, she that was great among the nations! She that was a princess among the provinces has become a vassal.
text.Rotherham: How is seated alone, the city that abounded with people, hath become as a widow,—She who abounded among the nations was a princess among provinces, hath come under tribute.
text.KJV: How doth the city sit solitary, that was full of people! how is she become as a widow! she that was great among the nations, and princess among the provinces, how is she become tributary!
text.NABRE: She who was great among the nations is now like a widow. Once a princess among the provinces, now a toiling slave.
text.DRB: How doth the city full of people, sit solitary: how is the lady of the Gentiles become as a widow: the princess of Provinces is made tributary?

text.NRSV: She weeps bitterly in the night, with tears on her cheeks; among all her lovers she has no one to comfort her; all her friends have dealt treacherously with her, they have become her enemies.
text.Rotherham: She, weepeth sore, in the night, and, her tear, is on her cheek, She hath none to comfort her, of all her lovers,—All her friends, have betrayed her, have become her foes.
text.KJV: She weepeth sore in the night, and her tears are on her cheeks: among all her lovers she hath none to comfort her: all her friends have dealt treacherously with her, they are become her enemies.
text.NABRE: She weeps incessantly in the night, her cheeks damp with tears. She has no one to comfort her from all her lovers; Her friends have all betrayed her, and become her enemies.
text.DRB: Weeping she hath wept in the night, and her tears are on her cheeks: there is none to comfort her of all her dear ones: all her friends have despised her, and are become her enemies.

text.NRSV: Judah has gone into exile with suffering and hard servitude; she lives now among the nations, and finds no resting place; her pursuers have all overtaken her in the midst of her distress.
text.Rotherham: Carried away captive is Judah—because of oppression, and because of great servitude, She, hath remained among the nations, hath found no place of rest,—All her pursuers, have overtaken her, between straits.
text.KJV: Judah is gone into captivity because of affliction, and because of great servitude: she dwelleth among the heathen, she findeth no rest: all her persecutors overtook her between the straits.
text.NABRE: Judah has gone into exile, after oppression and harsh labor; She dwells among the nations, yet finds no rest: All her pursuers overtake her in the narrow straits.
text.DRB: Judas is gone into transmigration because of affliction, and the multitude of bondage: she hath dwelt among the Gentiles, neither hath she found rest: all her persecuters have apprehended her within the straits.

text.NRSV: The roads to Zion mourn, for no one comes to the festivals; all her gates are desolate, her priests groan; her young girls grieve, and she her-self suffers bitterly.
text.Rotherham: The ways to Zion, are mourning, because none come to her appointed feasts, All her gates, are desolate, her priests, are sighing,—her virgins, are grieved, and, she, it is bitter for her.
text.KJV: The ways of Zion do mourn, because none come to the solemn feasts: all her gates are desolate: her priests sigh, her virgins are afflicted, and she is in bitterness.
text.NABRE: The roads to Zion mourn, empty of pilgrims to her feasts. All her gateways are desolate, her priests groan, Her young women grieve; her lot is bitter.
text.DRB: The ways of Sion mourn, because there are none that come to the solemnity: all her gates are destroyed: her priests sighing: her virgins loathsome, and herself is oppressed with bitterness.

text.NRSV: Her foes have become the masters, her enemies prosper, because the Lord has made her suffer for the multitude of her transgressions; her children have gone away, captives before the foe.
text.Rotherham: Her adversaries have become chief, her foes, are at ease, for, Yahweh, hath grieved her, because of the multitude of her transgressions,—Her children, have gone into captivity, before the adversary.
text.KJV: Her adversaries are the chief, her enemies prosper; for the Lord hath afflicted her for the multitude of her transgressions: her children are gone into captivity before the enemy.
text.NABRE: Her foes have come out on top, her enemies are secure; Because the Lord has afflicted her for her many rebellions. Her children have gone away, captive before the foe.
text.DRB: Her adversaries are made in the head, her enemies are enriched: because our Lord hath spoken upon her for the multitude of her iniquities: her little ones are led into captivity, before the face of the afflicter.

text.NRSV: From daughter Zion has departed all her majesty. Her princes have become like stags that find no pasture; they fled without strength before the pursuer.
text.Rotherham: Thus hath gone forth from the daughter of Zion, all that adorned her,—Her princes have become like harts that have found no pasture, and have gone strengthless before the pursuer.
text.KJV: And from the daughter of Zion all her beauty is departed: her princes are become like harts that find no pasture, and they are gone without strength before the pursuer.
text.NABRE: From daughter Zion has gone all her glory: Her princes have become like rams that find no pasture. They have gone off exhausted before their pursuers.
text.DRB: And from the daughter of Sion all her beauty is departed: her princes are become as rams not finding pastures: and they are gone without strength before the face of the pursuer.

text.NRSV: Jerusalem remembers, in the days of her affliction and wandering, all the precious things that were hers in days of old. When her people fell into the hand of the foe, and there was no one to help her, her foes looked on her; they mocked at her downfall.
text.Rotherham: Jerusalem hath remembered in the days of her humiliation and her fleeings—all her precious things, which have existed from the days of old,—Now that her people have been falling into the hand of the adversary, with none to help her, the adversaries have seen her, have mocked over her sabbath-keepings.
text.KJV: Jerusalem remembered in the days of her affliction and of her miseries all her pleasant things that she had in the days of old, when her people fell into the hand of the enemy, and none did help her: the adversaries saw her, and did mock at her sabbaths.
text.NABRE: Jerusalem remembers in days of wretched homelessness, All the precious things she once had in days gone by. But when her people fell into the hands of the foe, and she had no help, Her foes looked on and laughed at her collapse.
text.DRB: Jerusalem hath remembered the days of her affliction, and prevarication of all her things worthy to be desired, which she had from the days of old, when her people fell in the enemy's hand, and there was no helper: the enemies have seen her, and have scorned her sabbaths.

text.NRSV: Jerusalem sinned grievously, so she has become a mockery; all who honored her despise her, for they have seen her nakedness; she herself groans, and turns her face away.
text.Rotherham: Jerusalem, hath grievously sinned, For this cause, unto exile, hath she been delivered,—All who used to honour her, have despised her, for they have descried her unseemliness, yea, she herself, hath sighed, and turned back.
text.KJV: Jerusalem hath grievously sinned; therefore she is removed: all that honoured her despise her, because they have seen her nakedness: yea, she sigheth, and turneth backward.
text.NABRE: Jerusalem has sinned grievously, therefore she has become a mockery; Those who honored her now demean her, for they saw her nakedness; She herself groans out loud, and turns away.
text.DRB: Jerusalem hath sinned a sin, therefore is she made unstable: all that did glorify her, have despised her, because they have seen her ignominy: but she sighing is turned backward.

text.NRSV: Her uncleanness was in her skirts; she gave no thought to her future; her downfall was appalling, with no one to comfort her. “O Lord, behold my affliction, for the enemy has triumphed!”
text.Rotherham: Her impurity, is in her skirts, She hath not remembered her hereafter, Therefore hath she come down wonderfully, none to comfort her,—Behold, O Yahweh, my humiliation, that the foe, hath made himself great.
text.KJV: Her filthiness is in her skirts; she remembereth not her last end; therefore she came down wonderfully: she had no comforter. O Lord, behold my affliction: for the enemy hath magnified himself.
text.NABRE: Her uncleanness is on her skirt; she has no thought of her future. Her downfall is astonishing, with no one to comfort her. “Look, O Lord, at my misery; how the enemy triumphs!”
text.DRB: Her filthiness is on her feet, neither hath she remembered her end: she is pulled down exceedingly, not having a comforter: see o Lord mine affliction, because the enemy is exalted.

text.NRSV: Enemies have stretched out their hands over all her precious things; she has even seen the nations invade her sanctuary, those whom you forbade to enter your congregation.
text.Rotherham: His hand, hath the adversary spread out, over all her precious things,—for she saw that, the nations, entered her sanctuary, as to whom thou didst command they should not enter—in the convocation—unto thee!
text.KJV: The adversary hath spread out his hand upon all her pleasant things: for she hath seen that the heathen entered into her sanctuary, whom thou didst command that they should not enter into thy congregation.
text.NABRE: The foe stretched out his hands to all her precious things; She has seen the nations enter her sanctuary, Those you forbade to come into your assembly.
text.DRB: The enemy hath thrust his hand to all her things worthy to be desired: because she hath seen the Gentiles enter into her sanctuary, of whom thou gavest commandment that they should not enter into thy church.

text.NRSV: All her people groan as they search for bread; they trade their treasures for food to revive their strength. “Look, O Lord, and see how worthless I have become.”
text.Rotherham: All her people, are sighing, seeking bread, They have given their precious things for food, to bring back life,—Behold, O Yahweh, and discern, that I have become worthless.
text.KJV: All her people sigh, they seek bread; they have given their pleasant things for meat to relieve the soul: see, O Lord, and consider; for I am become vile.
text.NABRE: All her people groan, searching for bread; They give their precious things for food, to retain the breath of life. “Look, O Lord, and pay attention to how I have been demeaned!
text.DRB: All her people sighing, and seeking bread: they have given all precious things for meat to refresh the soul, see o Lord and consider, because I am become vile.

text.NRSV: Is it nothing to you, all you who pass by? Look and see if there is any sorrow like my sorrow, which was brought upon me, which the Lord inflicted on the day of his fierce anger.
text.Rotherham: Is it nothing to you, all ye that pass by? Look around and see, whether there is pain like my pain, which is severely dealt out to me,—in that Yahweh, hath caused grief, in the day of the glow of his anger?
text.KJV: Is it nothing to you, all ye that pass by? behold, and see if there be any sorrow like unto my sorrow, which is done unto me, wherewith the Lord hath afflicted me in the day of his fierce anger.
text.NABRE: Come, all who pass by the way, pay attention and see: Is there any pain like my pain, which has been ruthlessly inflicted upon me, With which the Lord has tormented me on the day of his blazing wrath?
text.DRB: O all ye that pass by the way, attend, and see if there be sorrow like to my sorrow: because he hath made vintage of me, as our Lord hath spoken in the day of the wrath of his fury.

text.NRSV: From on high he sent fire; it went deep into my bones; he spread a net for my feet; he turned me back; he has left me stunned, faint all day long.
text.Rotherham: From on high, sent he fire, among my bones, and laid them prostrate,—He spread out a net for my feet, he made me turn back, he made me desolate, all the day, faint.
text.KJV: From above hath he sent fire into my bones, and it prevaileth against them: he hath spread a net for my feet, he hath turned me back: he hath made me desolate and faint all the day.
text.NABRE: From on high he hurled fire down into my very bones; He spread out a net for my feet, and turned me back. He has left me desolate, in misery all day long.
text.DRB: From on high he hath cast a fire in my bones, and hath taught me: he hath spread a net for my feet, he hath turned me backward: he hath made me desolate, all the day consumed with sorrow.

text.NRSV: My transgressions were bound into a yoke; by his hand they were fastened together; they weigh on my neck, sapping my strength; the Lord delivered me into the hands of those whom I cannot withstand.
text.Rotherham: The yoke of my transgressions, hath been bound, by his hand—They have intertwined themselves, have come up on my neck, it hath paralysed my strength,—My Lord, hath delivered me, into the hands of those against whom I cannot rise up.
text.KJV: The yoke of my transgressions is bound by his hand: they are wreathed, and come up upon my neck: he hath made my strength to fall, the Lord hath delivered me into their hands, from whom I am not able to rise up.
text.NABRE: The yoke of my rebellions is bound together, fastened by his hand. His yoke is upon my neck; he has made my strength fail. The Lord has delivered me into the grip of those I cannot resist.
text.DRB: The yoke of mine iniquities hath watched: they are folded together in his hand, and put upon my neck: my strength is weakened: our Lord hath given me into the hand, from which I cannot rise.

text.NRSV: The Lord has rejected all my warriors in the midst of me; he proclaimed a time against me to crush my young men; the Lord has trodden as in a wine press the virgin daughter Judah.
text.Rotherham: My Lord, hath flouted at all my magnates, in my midst, He hath called against me a host, to crush my young men,—A winepress, hath My Lord trodden, to the virgin, the daughter of Judah.
text.KJV: The Lord hath trodden under foot all my mighty men in the midst of me: he hath called an assembly against me to crush my young men: the Lord hath trodden the virgin, the daughter of Judah, as in a winepress.
text.NABRE: All my valiant warriors my Lord has cast away; He proclaimed a feast against me to crush my young men; My Lord has trodden in the wine press virgin daughter Judah.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath taken away all my magnifical ones out of the midst of me: he hath called a time against me, to destroy mine elect: our Lord hath trodden the winepress to the virgin the daughter of Juda.

text.NRSV: For these things I weep; my eyes flow with tears; for a comforter is far from me, one to revive my courage; my children are desolate, for the enemy has prevailed.
text.Rotherham: For these things, am, I, weeping, Mine eye, mine eye, is running down with waters, for, far from me, is any who could comfort, could bring back my life,—My sons are amazed, for strong is the foe.
text.KJV: For these things I weep; mine eye, mine eye runneth down with water, because the comforter that should relieve my soul is far from me: my children are desolate, because the enemy prevailed.
text.NABRE: For these things I weep—My eyes! My eyes! They stream with tears! How far from me is anyone to comfort, anyone to restore my life. My children are desolate; the enemy has prevailed.”
text.DRB: Therefore am I weeping, and mine eye shedding tears: because a comforter is made far from me, converting my soul: my children are become desolate because the enemy hath prevailed.

text.NRSV: Zion stretches out her hands, but there is no one to comfort her; the Lord has commanded against Jacob that his neighbors should be his foes; Jerusalem has become a filthy thing among them.
text.Rotherham: Zion, hath spread forth, her hands, there is none to comfort her, Yahweh, hath given command, respecting Jacob, unto them who surround him—his adversaries,—Jerusalem, hath become as a removed woman, in their midst.
text.KJV: Zion spreadeth forth her hands, and there is none to comfort her: the Lord hath commanded concerning Jacob, that his adversaries should be round about him: Jerusalem is as a menstruous woman among them.
text.NABRE: Zion stretches out her hands, with no one to comfort her; The Lord has ordered against Jacob his foes all around; Jerusalem has become in their midst a thing unclean.
text.DRB: Sion hath spread forth her hands, there is none to comfort her: our Lord hath commanded against Jacob, round about him are his enemies: Jerusalem is become as a woman polluted with menstruous flowers among them.

text.NRSV: The Lord is in the right, for I have rebelled against his word; but hear, all you peoples, and behold my suffering; my young women and my young men have gone into captivity.
text.Rotherham: Righteous is Yahweh, for, against his bidding, had I rebelled,—Hear, I pray you, all ye peoples, and see my pain, My virgins and my young men, have gone into exile.
text.KJV: The Lord is righteous; for I have rebelled against his commandment: hear, I pray you, all people, and behold my sorrow: my virgins and my young men are gone into captivity.
text.NABRE: “The Lord is in the right; I had defied his command. Listen, all you peoples, and see my pain: My young women and young men have gone into captivity.
text.DRB: Our Lord is just, because I have provoked his mouth to wrath: hear I beseech all ye peoples, and see my sorrow: my virgins, and my young men are gone into captivity.

text.NRSV: I called to my lovers but they deceived me; my priests and elders perished in the city while seeking food to revive their strength.
text.Rotherham: I called to my lovers, they, deceived me, My priests and mine elders, in the city, have breathed their last,—for they sought them food, that they might bring back their life.
text.KJV: I called for my lovers, but they deceived me: my priests and mine elders gave up the ghost in the city, while they sought their meat to relieve their souls.
text.NABRE: I cried out to my lovers, but they failed me. My priests and my elders perished in the city; How desperately they searched for food, to save their lives!
text.DRB: I have called my friends, they have deceived me: my priests and my ancients are consumed in the city: because they have sought meat for themselves, to refresh their soul.

text.NRSV: See, O Lord, how distressed I am; my stomach churns, my heart is wrung within me, because I have been very rebellious. In the street the sword bereaves; in the house it is like death.
text.Rotherham: See, O Yahweh, that I am in distress, Mine inward parts, are in ferment, My heart is turned within me, for I have obstinately rebelled,—Without, bereaveth the sword, Within, is like death,
text.KJV: Behold, O Lord; for I am in distress: my bowels are troubled; mine heart is turned within me; for I have grievously rebelled: abroad the sword bereaveth, at home there is as death.
text.NABRE: Look, O Lord, at the anguish I suffer! My stomach churns, And my heart recoils within me: How bitter I am! Outside the sword bereaves— indoors, there is death.
text.DRB: See o Lord that I am in tribulation, my belly is troubled: my heart is overturned in myself, because I am full of bitterness: the sword killeth abroad, and at home it is like death.

text.NRSV: They heard how I was groaning, with no one to comfort me. All my enemies heard of my trouble; they are glad that you have done it. Bring on the day you have announced, and let them be as I am.
text.Rotherham: They have heard—that, sighing am, I, [and am saying] —There is none to comfort me, All my foes,—having heard of my calamity—have rejoiced, because, thou, hast done it,—thou hast brought in the day thou didst proclaim, So let them become like me.
text.KJV: They have heard that I sigh: there is none to comfort me: all mine enemies have heard of my trouble; they are glad that thou hast done it: thou wilt bring the day that thou hast called, and they shall be like unto me.
text.NABRE: Hear how I am groaning; there is no one to comfort me. All my enemies hear of my misery and rejoice over what you have done. Bring on the day you proclaimed, and let them become like me!
text.DRB: They have heard that I do sigh, and there is none to comfort me: all mine enemies have heard mine evil, they have rejoiced, because thou hast done it: thou hast brought a day of consolation, and they shall be made like to me.

text.NRSV: Let all their evil doing come before you; and deal with them as you have dealt with me because of all my transgressions; for my groans are many and my heart is faint.
text.Rotherham: Let all their wickedness, come in, before thee, and deal thou severely with them, according as thou hast dealt severely with me, for all my transgressions; for many are my sighs, and, my heart, is sick.
text.KJV: Let all their wickedness come before thee; and do unto them, as thou hast done unto me for all my transgressions: for my sighs are many, and my heart is faint.
text.NABRE: Let all their evil come before you and deal with them As you have so ruthlessly dealt with me for all my rebellions. My groans are many, my heart is sick.”
text.DRB: Let all their evil enter in before thee: and vintage them, as thou hast vintaged me for all mine iniquities: for my sighings are many, and my heart is sorrowful.

text.NRSV: How the Lord in his anger has humiliated daughter Zion! He has thrown down from heaven to earth the splendor of Israel; he has not remembered his footstool in the day of his anger.
text.Rotherham: How could My Lord, in his anger, enshroud in gloom, the daughter of Zion? have cast from the heavens to the earth, the beauty of Israel? and not have remembered his footstool, in the day of his anger?
text.KJV: How hath the Lord covered the daughter of Zion with a cloud in his anger, and cast down from heaven unto the earth the beauty of Israel, and remembered not his footstool in the day of his anger!
text.NABRE: How the Lord in his wrath has abhorred daughter Zion, Casting down from heaven to earth the glory of Israel, Not remembering his footstool on the day of his wrath!
text.DRB: How hath our Lord in his fury covered the daughter of Sion with darkness: cast forth the noble one of Israel from heaven to the earth, and hath not remembered the footstool of his feet in the day of his fury.

text.NRSV: The Lord has destroyed without mercy all the dwellings of Jacob; in his wrath he has broken down the strongholds of daughter Judah; he has brought low in dishonor the kingdom and its rulers.
text.Rotherham: My Lord hath swallowed up—without pity—all the pastures of Jacob, hath laid waste—in his indignation—the strongholds of the daughter of Judah, hath brought them down to the ground,—hath profaned the kingdom, and the princes thereof.
text.KJV: The Lord hath swallowed up all the habitations of Jacob, and hath not pitied: he hath thrown down in his wrath the strong holds of the daughter of Judah; he hath brought them down to the ground: he hath polluted the kingdom and the princes thereof.
text.NABRE: The Lord has devoured without pity all of Jacob’s dwellings; In his fury he has razed daughter Judah’s defenses, Has brought to the ground in dishonor a kingdom and its princes.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath cast down headlong, and hath not spared, all the beautiful things of Jacob: he hath destroyed in his fury the munitions of the virgin of Juda, and cast it down to the ground: he hath polluted the kingdom, and the princes thereof.

text.NRSV: He has cut down in fierce anger all the might of Israel; he has withdrawn his right hand from them in the face of the enemy; he has burned like a flaming fire in Jacob, consuming all around.
text.Rotherham: He hath broken off—in the glow of his anger—the whole horn of Israel, hath turned back his right hand, from the face of the foe,—and hath kindled against Jacob, a very fire of flame, devouring round about.
text.KJV: He hath cut off in his fierce anger all the horn of Israel: he hath drawn back his right hand from before the enemy, and he burned against Jacob like a flaming fire, which devoureth round about.
text.NABRE: In blazing wrath, he cut down entirely the horn of Israel; He withdrew the support of his right hand when the enemy approached; He burned against Jacob like a blazing fire that consumes everything in its path.
text.DRB: He hath broken every horn of Israel in the wrath of fury: he hath turned away his right hand backward from the face of the enemy: and he hath kindled in Jacob as it were the fire of a flame devouring round about.

text.NRSV: He has bent his bow like an enemy, with his right hand set like a foe; he has killed all in whom we took pride in the tent of daughter Zion; he has poured out his fury like fire.
text.Rotherham: He hath trodden his bow like a foe, his right hand erect as an adversary, and hath slain all them who delighted the eye,—In the home of the daughter of Zion, hath he poured out, as fire, his indignation.
text.KJV: He hath bent his bow like an enemy: he stood with his right hand as an adversary, and slew all that were pleasant to the eye in the tabernacle of the daughter of Zion: he poured out his fury like fire.
text.NABRE: He bent his bow like an enemy; the arrow in his right hand Like a foe, he killed all those held precious; On the tent of daughter Zion he poured out his wrath like fire.
text.DRB: He hath bent his bow as an enemy, he hath fastened his right hand as an adversary: and he hast killed all, that was fair to behold in the tabernacle of the daughter of Sion, he hath poured out his indignation as fire.

text.NRSV: The Lord has become like an enemy; he has destroyed Israel; he has destroyed all its palaces, laid in ruins its strongholds; and he has multiplied in daughter Judah mourning and lamentation.
text.Rotherham: My Lord hath become like a foe, hath swallowed up Israel, hath swallowed up all her castles, ruined his strongholds,—and hath caused to abound, in the daughter of Judah, lamentation and mourning.
text.KJV: The Lord was as an enemy: he hath swallowed up Israel, he hath swallowed up all her palaces: he hath destroyed his strong holds, and hath increased in the daughter of Judah mourning and lamentation.
text.NABRE: The Lord has become the enemy, he has devoured Israel: Devoured all its strongholds, destroyed its defenses, Multiplied moaning and groaning throughout daughter Judah.
text.DRB: Our Lord is become as an enemy: he hath cast down Israel headlong, he hath cast down headlong all her walls: he hath destroyed the munitions thereof, and hath replenished in the daughter of Juda the humbled man and humbled woman.

text.NRSV: He has broken down his booth like a garden, he has destroyed his tabernacle; the Lord has abolished in Zion festival and sabbath, and in his fierce indignation has spurned king and priest.
text.Rotherham: Thus hath he destroyed, like a garden, his pavilion, hath laid waste his place of assembly,—Yahweh, hath caused to be forgotten, in Zion, the appointed assembly and the sabbath, and hath spurned, in the indignation of his anger, the king and the priest.
text.KJV: And he hath violently taken away his tabernacle, as if it were of a garden: he hath destroyed his places of the assembly: the Lord hath caused the solemn feasts and sabbaths to be forgotten in Zion, and hath despised in the indignation of his anger the king and the priest.
text.NABRE: He laid waste his booth like a garden, destroyed his shrine; The Lord has blotted out in Zion feast day and sabbath, Has scorned in fierce wrath king and priest.
text.DRB: And he hath destroyed his tent as a garden, he hath thrown down his tabernacle: our Lord hath brought festivity and sabbath in Sion to oblivion: and king and priest into reproach, and into the indignation of his fury.

text.NRSV: The Lord has scorned his altar, disowned his sanctuary; he has delivered into the hand of the enemy the walls of her palaces; a clamor was raised in the house of the Lord as on a day of festival.
text.Rotherham: My Lord hath rejected his altar, hath abhorred his sanctuary, hath delivered, into the hand of the foe, the walls of her castles,—A voice, have they uttered in the house of Yahweh, as on the day of an appointed assembly.
text.KJV: The Lord hath cast off his altar, he hath abhorred his sanctuary, he hath given up into the hand of the enemy the walls of her palaces; they have made a noise in the house of the Lord, as in the day of a solemn feast.
text.NABRE: The Lord has rejected his altar, spurned his sanctuary; He has handed over to the enemy the walls of its strongholds. They shout in the house of the Lord as on a feast day.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath rejected, he hath cursed his sanctification: he hath delivered the walls of the towers thereof into the hand of the enemy: they have made a noise in the house of our Lord, as in a solemn day.

text.NRSV: The Lord determined to lay in ruins the wall of daughter Zion; he stretched the line; he did not stay his hand from destroying; he caused rampart and wall to lament; they languish together.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh hath devised to lay in ruins, the wall of the daughter of Zion, he hath stretched out a line, he hath not turned back his hand from swallowing up,—Thus hath he caused to mourn—rampart and wall, together, have they languished!
text.KJV: The Lord hath purposed to destroy the wall of the daughter of Zion: he hath stretched out a line, he hath not withdrawn his hand from destroying: therefore he made the rampart and the wall to lament; they languished together.
text.NABRE: The Lord was bent on destroying the wall of daughter Zion: He stretched out the measuring line; did not hesitate to devour, Brought grief on rampart and wall till both succumbed.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath meant to destroy the wall of the daughter of Sion: he hath stretched out his cord, and hath not turned away his hand from destruction: and the forewall hath mourned, and the wall is destroyed together.

text.NRSV: Her gates have sunk into the ground; he has ruined and broken her bars; her king and her princes are among the nations; guidance is no more, and her prophets obtain no vision from the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Her gates, have sunk in the earth, He hath destroyed and broken in pieces, her bars,—Her king and her princes, are among the nations, There is no instruction, Even her prophets, have found no vision, from Yahweh.
text.KJV: Her gates are sunk into the ground; he hath destroyed and broken her bars: her king and her princes are among the Gentiles: the law is no more; her prophets also find no vision from the Lord.
text.NABRE: Her gates sank into the ground; he smashed her bars to bits. Her king and her princes are among the nations; instruction is wanting, Even her prophets do not obtain any vision from the Lord.
text.DRB: Her gates are fastened in the ground: he hath destroyed, and broken her bars: her king and her princes in the Gentiles: there is no law, and her prophets have not found vision from our Lord.

text.NRSV: The elders of daughter Zion sit on the ground in silence; they have thrown dust on their heads and put on sackcloth; the young girls of Jerusalem have bowed their heads to the ground.
text.Rotherham: Seated on the ground, in silence, are the elders of the daughter of Zion, They have lifted up dust on their head, have girded themselves with sackcloth,—Bowed down to the ground is the head, of the virgins of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: The elders of the daughter of Zion sit upon the ground, and keep silence: they have cast up dust upon their heads; they have girded themselves with sackcloth: the virgins of Jerusalem hang down their heads to the ground.
text.NABRE: The elders of daughter Zion sit silently on the ground; They cast dust on their heads and dress in sackcloth; The young women of Jerusalem bow their heads to the ground.
text.DRB: The ancients of the daughter of Sion have sitten on the ground, they have held their peace: they have sprinkled their heads with dust, they are girded with hair cloths, the virgins of Jerusalem have cast down their heads to the ground.

text.NRSV: My eyes are spent with weeping; my stomach churns; my bile is poured out on the ground because of the destruction of my people, because infants and babes faint in the streets of the city.
text.Rotherham: Blinded with tears are mine eyes, In ferment is my body, Poured out to the earth is my grief, for the sore hurt of the daughter of my people,—when child and suckling are swooning, in the broadways of the city.
text.KJV: Mine eyes do fail with tears, my bowels are troubled, my liver is poured upon the earth, for the destruction of the daughter of my people; because the children and the sucklings swoon in the streets of the city.
text.NABRE: My eyes are spent with tears, my stomach churns; My bile is poured out on the ground at the brokenness of the daughter of my people, As children and infants collapse in the streets of the town.
text.DRB: Mine eyes have failed for tears, my bowels are troubled: my liver is poured out on the earth, for the destruction of the daughter of my people, when the little one, and the sucking fainted in the streets of the town.

text.NRSV: They cry to their mothers, “Where is bread and wine?” as they faint like the wounded in the streets of the city, as their life is poured out on their mothers’ bosom.
text.Rotherham: To their mothers, they keep saying, Where are corn and wine? Swooning off, like one thrust through, in the broadways of the city, pouring out their life into the bosom of their mothers.
text.KJV: They say to their mothers, Where is corn and wine? when they swooned as the wounded in the streets of the city, when their soul was poured out into their mothers’ bosom.
text.NABRE: They cry out to their mothers, “Where is bread and wine?” As they faint away like the wounded in the streets of the city, As their life is poured out in their mothers’ arms.
text.DRB: They said to their mothers: Where is wheat and wine? When they fainted as the wounded in the streets of the city: when they yielded up the ghosts in the bosom of their mothers.

text.NRSV: What can I say for you, to what compare you, O daughter Jerusalem? To what can I liken you, that I may comfort you, O virgin daughter Zion? For vast as the sea is your ruin; who can heal you?
text.Rotherham: How shall I solemnly admonish thee? What shall I liken to thee, thou daughter of Jerusalem? What shall I compare with thee, that I may comfort thee, thou virgin, daughter of Zion? for, great as the sea, is thy grievous injury, Who can heal thee?
text.KJV: What thing shall I take to witness for thee? what thing shall I liken to thee, O daughter of Jerusalem? what shall I equal to thee, that I may comfort thee, O virgin daughter of Zion? for thy breach is great like the sea: who can heal thee?
text.NABRE: To what can I compare you —to what can I liken you— O daughter Jerusalem? What example can I give in order to comfort you, virgin daughter Zion? For your breach is vast as the sea; who could heal you?
text.DRB: Whereto shall I compare thee? Or whereto shall I liken thee o daughter of Jerusalem: whereto shall I make thee equal, and comfort thee o virgin daughter of Sion? For great is thy destruction as the sea: who shall heal thee?

text.NRSV: Your prophets have seen for you false and deceptive visions; they have not exposed your iniquity to restore your fortunes, but have seen oracles for you that are false and misleading.
text.Rotherham: Thy prophets, have had visions for thee, false and foolish, and have not unveiled thine iniquity, to turn back thy captivity,—Yea they have had visions for thee, oracles of falsehood, and enticements!
text.KJV: Thy prophets have seen vain and foolish things for thee: and they have not discovered thine iniquity, to turn away thy captivity; but have seen for thee false burdens and causes of banishment.
text.NABRE: Your prophets provided you visions of whitewashed illusion; They did not lay bare your guilt, in order to restore your fortunes; They saw for you only oracles of empty deceit.
text.DRB: Thy prophets have seen false and foolish things for thee: neither have they opened thine iniquity, to provoke thee to penance, but they have seen false burdens and banishments for thee.

text.NRSV: All who pass by snap their fingers at you; they hiss and wag their heads at daughter Jerusalem; “Is this the city that was called the perfection of beauty, the joy of all the earth?”
text.Rotherham: All passing by, have clapped, over thee, their hands, have hissed and wagged their head over the daughter of Jerusalem,—[saying], Is, this, the city, of which men used to say—The perfection of beauty! A joy to the whole earth!
text.KJV: All that pass by clap their hands at thee; they hiss and wag their head at the daughter of Jerusalem, saying, Is this the city that men call The perfection of beauty, The joy of the whole earth?
text.NABRE: All who pass by on the road, clap their hands at you; They hiss and wag their heads over daughter Jerusalem: “Is this the city they used to call perfect in beauty and joy of all the earth?”
text.DRB: All that passed by the way have clapped their hands upon thee: they have hissed, and moved their head upon the daughter of Jerusalem, saying: Is this the city of perfect beauty, the joy of all the earth?

text.NRSV: All your enemies open their mouths against you; they hiss, they gnash their teeth, they cry: “We have devoured her! Ah, this is the day we longed for; at last we have seen it!”
text.Rotherham: All thy foes, have opened wide, over thee their mouth, They have hissed, and gnashed their teeth, They have said—We have swallowed [her] up! Surely, this, is the day for which we have waited, We have found! We have seen!
text.KJV: All thine enemies have opened their mouth against thee: they hiss and gnash the teeth: they say, We have swallowed her up: certainly this is the day that we looked for; we have found, we have seen it.
text.NABRE: They open their mouths against you, all your enemies; They hiss and gnash their teeth, saying, “We have devoured her! How we have waited for this day— we have lived to see it!”
text.DRB: All thine enemies have opened their mouth upon thee: they have hissed, and gnashed with the teeth, and have said: We will devour: Lo this is the day, which we expected: we have found it, we have seen it.

text.NRSV: The Lord has done what he purposed, he has carried out his threat; as he ordained long ago, he has demolished without pity; he has made the enemy rejoice over you, and exalted the might of your foes.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh hath done what he thought, hath carried out his word, wherewith he gave charge in the days of old, hath thrown down, and not spared,—Thus hath he let the enemy rejoice over thee, hath raised high the horn of thine adversaries.
text.KJV: The Lord hath done that which he had devised; he hath fulfilled his word that he had commanded in the days of old: he hath thrown down, and hath not pitied: and he hath caused thine enemy to rejoice over thee, he hath set up the horn of thine adversaries.
text.NABRE: The Lord has done what he planned. He has fulfilled the threat Decreed from days of old, destroying without pity! He let the enemy gloat over you and exalted the horn of your foes.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath done the things that he meant, he hath accomplished his word, which he commanded from the days of old: he hath destroyed, and hath not spared, and he hath made the enemy joyful over thee, and hath exalted the horn of thine adversaries.

text.NRSV: Cry aloud to the Lord! O wall of daughter Zion! Let tears stream down like a torrent day and night! Give yourself no rest, your eyes no respite!
text.Rotherham: Their heart, hath made outcry, unto My Lord,—O wall of the daughter of Zion—Let tears run down as a torrent day and night, Do not give thyself relief, Let not the weeping of thine eye cease!
text.KJV: Their heart cried unto the Lord, O wall of the daughter of Zion, let tears run down like a river day and night: give thyself no rest; let not the apple of thine eye cease.
text.NABRE: Cry out to the Lord from your heart, wall of daughter Zion! Let your tears flow like a torrent day and night; Give yourself no rest, no relief for your eyes.
text.DRB: Their heart hath cried to our Lord upon the walls of the daughter of Sion: Shed tears as a torrent by day, and night: give no rest to thyself, neither let the apple of thine eye cease.

text.NRSV: Arise, cry out in the night, at the beginning of the watches! Pour out your heart like water before the presence of the Lord! Lift your hands to him for the lives of your children, who faint for hunger at the head of every street.
text.Rotherham: Arise, cry out in the night, At the beginning of the watches, pour out, like waters, thy heart, right before the face of My Lord,—Lift up, above thee, the palms of thy hands, for the life of thy children, who are swooning for hunger, at the top of all the streets!
text.KJV: Arise, cry out in the night: in the beginning of the watches pour out thine heart like water before the face of the Lord: lift up thy hands toward him for the life of thy young children, that faint for hunger in the top of every street.
text.NABRE: Rise up! Wail in the night, at the start of every watch; Pour out your heart like water before the Lord; Lift up your hands to him for the lives of your children, Who collapse from hunger at the corner of every street.
text.DRB: Arise, praise in the night in the beginning of the watches: pour out thy heart as waters before the sight of our Lord: lift up thy hands to him for the life of thy little ones, which have fainted for famine in the head of all high ways.

text.NRSV: Look, O Lord, and consider! To whom have you done this? Should women eat their offspring, the children they have borne? Should priest and prophet be killed in the sanctuary of the Lord?
text.Rotherham: See, O Yahweh, and do consider, to whom thou hast acted thus severely,—Will women, devour, their own fruit—the children they have dandled? Shall priest and prophet, be slain in the sanctuary of My Lord?
text.KJV: Behold, O Lord, and consider to whom thou hast done this. Shall the women eat their fruit, and children of a span long? shall the priest and the prophet be slain in the sanctuary of the Lord?
text.NABRE: “Look, O Lord, and pay attention: to whom have you been so ruthless? Must women eat their own offspring, the very children they have borne? Are priest and prophet to be slain in the sanctuary of the Lord?
text.DRB: See o Lord, and consider whom thou hast vintaged thus: shall women then eat their own fruit, little ones of the measure of a span? Is the priest, and the prophet slain in the sanctuary of our Lord?

text.NRSV: The young and the old are lying on the ground in the streets; my young women and my young men have fallen by the sword; in the day of your anger you have killed them, slaughtering without pity.
text.Rotherham: Youth and elder, have lain down on the ground in the streets, My virgins and my young men, have fallen by the sword,—Thou hast slain, in the day of thine anger, Thou hast slaughtered, hast not spared!
text.KJV: The young and the old lie on the ground in the streets: my virgins and my young men are fallen by the sword; thou hast slain them in the day of thine anger; thou hast killed, and not pitied.
text.NABRE: They lie on the ground in the streets, young and old alike; Both my young women and young men are cut down by the sword; You killed them on the day of your wrath, slaughtered without pity.
text.DRB: The child and the old man lay on the ground without: my virgins and my youngmen are fallen by the sword: thou hast killed in the day of thy fury: thou hast strucken, neither hast thou had mercy.

text.NRSV: You summoned, as if to a festival day, my terrors from every side; and on the day of the Lord’s anger no one escaped or survived; those whom I bore and reared my enemy has destroyed.
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou proclaim, like the day of an appointed meeting—My terrors round about? when there was not—in the day of the anger of Yahweh—fugitive or survivor,—Those whom I dandled and reared, my foe hath destroyed.
text.KJV: Thou hast called as in a solemn day my terrors round about, so that in the day of the Lord’s anger none escaped nor remained: those that I have swaddled and brought up hath mine enemy consumed.
text.NABRE: You summoned as to a feast day terrors on every side; On the day of the Lord ’s wrath, none survived or escaped. Those I have borne and nurtured, my enemy has utterly destroyed.”
text.DRB: Thou hast called as it were to a solemn day, those that should terrify me round about, and there was none in the day of the fury of our Lord, that escaped and was left: whom I brought up, & nourished, mine enemy hath consumed them.

text.NRSV: I am one who has seen affliction under the rod of God’s wrath;
text.Rotherham: I, am the man, that hath seen affliction, by the rod of his indignation;
text.KJV: I am the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.
text.NABRE: I am one who has known affliction under the rod of God’s anger,
text.DRB: The man that see my poverty in the rod of his indignation.

text.NRSV: he has driven and brought me into darkness without any light;
text.Rotherham: Me, hath he driven out and brought into darkness, and not light;
text.KJV: He hath led me, and brought me into darkness, but not into light.
text.NABRE: One whom he has driven and forced to walk in darkness, not in light;
text.DRB: He hath led me, and brought me into darkness and not into light.

text.NRSV: against me alone he turns his hand, again and again, all day long.
text.Rotherham: Surely, against me, doth he again and again, turn his hand all the day.
text.KJV: Surely against me is he turned; he turneth his hand against me all the day.
text.NABRE: Against me alone he turns his hand— again and again all day long.
text.DRB: Only against me he hath turned and hath converted his hand all the day.

text.NRSV: He has made my flesh and my skin waste away, and broken my bones;
text.Rotherham: He hath worn out my flesh and my skin, hath broken my bones;
text.KJV: My flesh and my skin hath he made old; he hath broken my bones.
text.NABRE: He has worn away my flesh and my skin, he has broken my bones;
text.DRB: He hath made my skin old and my flesh, he hath broken my bones.

text.NRSV: he has besieged and enveloped me with bitterness and tribulation;
text.Rotherham: He hath built up against me, and carried round me, fortifications and a trench;
text.KJV: He hath builded against me, and compassed me with gall and travail.
text.NABRE: He has besieged me all around with poverty and hardship;
text.DRB: He hath built round about me, and he hath compassed me with gall and labour.

text.NRSV: he has made me live in darkness like the dead of long ago.
text.Rotherham: In dark places, hath he made me sit, like the dead of age-past times.
text.KJV: He hath set me in dark places, as they that be dead of old.
text.NABRE: He has left me to dwell in dark places like those long dead.
text.DRB: In dark places he hath placed me as the everlasting dead.

text.NRSV: He has walled me about so that I cannot escape; he has put heavy chains on me;
text.Rotherham: He hath walled up around me, that I cannot get out, hath weighted my fetter;
text.KJV: He hath hedged me about, that I cannot get out: he hath made my chain heavy.
text.NABRE: He has hemmed me in with no escape, weighed me down with chains;
text.DRB: He hath built round about against me, that I go not forth: he hath aggravated my fetters.

text.NRSV: though I call and cry for help, he shuts out my prayer;
text.Rotherham: Yea, when I make outcry and implore, he hath shut out my prayer;
text.KJV: Also when I cry and shout, he shutteth out my prayer.
text.NABRE: Even when I cry for help, he stops my prayer;
text.DRB: Yea and when I shall cry, and ask, he hath excluded my prayer.

text.NRSV: he has blocked my ways with hewn stones, he has made my paths crooked.
text.Rotherham: He hath walled in my ways with hewn stone, my paths, hath he caused to wind back.
text.KJV: He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone, he hath made my paths crooked.
text.NABRE: He has hemmed in my ways with fitted stones, and made my paths crooked.
text.DRB: He hath shut up my ways with square stones, he hath subverted my paths.

text.NRSV: He is a bear lying in wait for me, a lion in hiding;
text.Rotherham: A bear lying in wait, he is to me, a lion, in secret places;
text.KJV: He was unto me as a bear lying in wait, and as a lion in secret places.
text.NABRE: He has been a bear lying in wait for me, a lion in hiding!
text.DRB: He is become unto me a bear lying in wait: a lion in secret places.

text.NRSV: he led me off my way and tore me to pieces; he has made me desolate;
text.Rotherham: My ways, hath he turned aside, and hath torn me in pieces, hath made me desolate;
text.KJV: He hath turned aside my ways, and pulled me in pieces: he hath made me desolate.
text.NABRE: He turned me aside and tore me apart, leaving me ravaged.
text.DRB: He hath subverted my paths, and hath broken me, he hath made me desolate.

text.NRSV: he bent his bow and set me as a mark for his arrow.
text.Rotherham: He hath trodden his bow, and set me up, as a mark for the arrow.
text.KJV: He hath bent his bow, and set me as a mark for the arrow.
text.NABRE: He bent his bow, and set me up as a target for his arrow.
text.DRB: He hath bent his bow, and set me as a mark for the arrow.

text.NRSV: He shot into my vitals the arrows of his quiver;
text.Rotherham: He hath caused to enter my reins, the sons of his quiver;
text.KJV: He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.
text.NABRE: He pierced my kidneys with shafts from his quiver.
text.DRB: He hath shot in my reins the daughters of his quiver.

text.NRSV: I have become the laughingstock of all my people, the object of their taunt-songs all day long.
text.Rotherham: I have become a derision to all my people, their song all the day;
text.KJV: I was a derision to all my people; and their song all the day.
text.NABRE: I have become a laughingstock to all my people, their taunt all day long;
text.DRB: I am made a derision to all my people, their song all the day.

text.NRSV: He has filled me with bitterness, he has sated me with wormwood.
text.Rotherham: He hath sated me with bitter things, hath drenched me with wormwood.
text.KJV: He hath filled me with bitterness, he hath made me drunken with wormwood.
text.NABRE: He has sated me with bitterness, filled me with wormwood.
text.DRB: He hath replenished me with bitterness, he hath inebriated me with wormwood.

text.NRSV: He has made my teeth grind on gravel, and made me cower in ashes;
text.Rotherham: And he hath crushed, with gravel-stones, my teeth, hath made me cower in ashes;
text.KJV: He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.
text.NABRE: He has made me eat gravel, trampled me into the dust;
text.DRB: And he hath broken my teeth by number, he hath fed me with ashes.

text.NRSV: my soul is bereft of peace; I have forgotten what happiness is;
text.Rotherham: And thou hast thrust away from welfare, my soul, I have forgotten prosperity;
text.KJV: And thou hast removed my soul far off from peace: I forgat prosperity.
text.NABRE: My life is deprived of peace, I have forgotten what happiness is;
text.DRB: And my soul is repelled from peace, I have forgotten good things.

text.NRSV: so I say, “Gone is my glory, and all that I had hoped for from the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And I said, Vanished is mine endurance, even mine expectation, from Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I said, My strength and my hope is perished from the Lord:
text.NABRE: My enduring hope, I said, has perished before the Lord.
text.DRB: And I said: Mine end is perished, and mine hope from our Lord.

text.NRSV: The thought of my affliction and my homelessness is wormwood and gall!
text.Rotherham: Remember my humiliation and my fleeings, the wormwood and poison;
text.KJV: Remembering mine affliction and my misery, the wormwood and the gall.
text.NABRE: The thought of my wretched homelessness is wormwood and poison;
text.DRB: Remember my poverty, and transgression, the wormwood, and the gall.

text.NRSV: My soul continually thinks of it and is bowed down within me.
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt, indeed remember, that, bowed down concerning myself, is my soul;
text.KJV: My soul hath them still in remembrance, and is humbled in me.
text.NABRE: Remembering it over and over, my soul is downcast.
text.DRB: Remembering I will be mindful, and my soul shall languish in me.

text.NRSV: But this I call to mind, and therefore I have hope:
text.Rotherham: This, will I bring back to my heart, therefore, will I hope.
text.KJV: This I recall to my mind, therefore have I hope.
text.NABRE: But this I will call to mind; therefore I will hope:
text.DRB: Recording this thing in my heart, therefore will I hope.

text.NRSV: The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases, his mercies never come to an end;
text.Rotherham: The lovingkindnesses of Yahweh, verily they are not exhausted, Verily! not at an end, are his compassions:
text.KJV: It is of the Lord’s mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not.
text.NABRE: The Lord ’s acts of mercy are not exhausted, his compassion is not spent;
text.DRB: The mercies of our Lord that we are not consumed: because his commiserations have not failed.

text.NRSV: they are new every morning; great is your faithfulness.
text.Rotherham: New things for the mornings! Abundant is thy faithfulness:
text.KJV: They are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness.
text.NABRE: They are renewed each morning— great is your faithfulness!
text.DRB: New in the morning, great is thy fidelity.

text.NRSV: “The Lord is my portion,” says my soul, “therefore I will hope in him.”
text.Rotherham: My portion, is Yahweh, saith my soul, For this cause, will I wait for him.
text.KJV: The Lord is my portion, saith my soul; therefore will I hope in him.
text.NABRE: The Lord is my portion, I tell myself, therefore I will hope in him.
text.DRB: Our Lord is my portion, said my soul: therefore will I expect him.

text.NRSV: The Lord is good to those who wait for him, to the soul that seeks him.
text.Rotherham: Good is Yahweh, to them who wait for him, to the soul that will seek him;
text.KJV: The Lord is good unto them that wait for him, to the soul that seeketh him.
text.NABRE: The Lord is good to those who trust in him, to the one that seeks him;
text.DRB: Our Lord is good to them that hope in him, to the soul that seeketh him.

text.NRSV: It is good that one should wait quietly for the salvation of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Good it is—both to wait and to be silent, for the deliverance of Yahweh;
text.KJV: It is good that a man should both hope and quietly wait for the salvation of the Lord.
text.NABRE: It is good to hope in silence for the Lord ’s deliverance.
text.DRB: It is good to wait with silence for the salvation of God.

text.NRSV: It is good for one to bear the yoke in youth,
text.Rotherham: Good it is for a man, that he should bear the yoke in his youth.
text.KJV: It is good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth.
text.NABRE: It is good for a person, when young, to bear the yoke,
text.DRB: It is good for a man, when he beareth the yoke from his youth.

text.NRSV: to sit alone in silence when the Lord has imposed it,
text.Rotherham: Let him sit alone, and keep silence, because he took it upon himself:
text.KJV: He sitteth alone and keepeth silence, because he hath borne it upon him.
text.NABRE: To sit alone and in silence, when its weight lies heavy,
text.DRB: He shall sit solitary, and hold his peace: because he hath lifted himself above himself.

text.NRSV: to put one’s mouth to the dust (there may yet be hope),
text.Rotherham: Let him put, in the dust, his mouth, peradventure, there is hope!
text.KJV: He putteth his mouth in the dust; if so be there may be hope.
text.NABRE: To put one’s mouth in the dust— there may yet be hope—
text.DRB: He shall put his mouth in the dust, if perhaps there be hope.

text.NRSV: to give one’s cheek to the smiter, and be filled with insults.
text.Rotherham: Let him give, to him that smiteth him, his cheek, let him be sated with reproach.
text.KJV: He giveth his cheek to him that smiteth him: he is filled full with reproach.
text.NABRE: To offer one’s cheek to be struck, to be filled with disgrace.
text.DRB: He shall give the cheek to him that striketh him, he shall be filled with reproaches.

text.NRSV: For the Lord will not reject forever.
text.Rotherham: Surely My Lord, will not cast off, unto times age-abiding;
text.KJV: For the Lord will not cast off for ever:
text.NABRE: For the Lord does not reject forever;
text.DRB: Because our Lord will not reject for ever.

text.NRSV: Although he causes grief, he will have compassion according to the abundance of his steadfast love;
text.Rotherham: Surely, though he cause grief, yet will he have compassion, according to the multitude of his lovingkindnesses;
text.KJV: But though he cause grief, yet will he have compassion according to the multitude of his mercies.
text.NABRE: Though he brings grief, he takes pity, according to the abundance of his mercy;
text.DRB: Because if he hath rejected, he will also have mercy, according to the multitude of his mercies.

text.NRSV: for he does not willingly afflict or grieve anyone.
text.Rotherham: Surely he hath not afflicted from his heart, nor caused sorrow to the sons of men.
text.KJV: For he doth not afflict willingly nor grieve the children of men.
text.NABRE: He does not willingly afflict or bring grief to human beings.
text.DRB: For he hath not humbled from his heart, and cast off the children of men.

text.NRSV: When all the prisoners of the land are crushed under foot,
text.Rotherham: To crush, under his feet, any of the prisoners of the earth;
text.KJV: To crush under his feet all the prisoners of the earth,
text.NABRE: That someone tramples underfoot all the prisoners in the land,
text.DRB: To stamp under his feet all the prisoners of the earth.

text.NRSV: when human rights are perverted in the presence of the Most High,
text.Rotherham: To turn aside the right of a man, before the face of the Most High;
text.KJV: To turn aside the right of a man before the face of the most High,
text.NABRE: Or denies justice to anyone in the very sight of the Most High,
text.DRB: To avert the judgement of a man before the face of the Highest

text.NRSV: when one’s case is subverted— does the Lord not see it?
text.Rotherham: To oppress a son of earth in his cause, My Lord, hath made no provision.
text.KJV: To subvert a man in his cause, the Lord approveth not.
text.NABRE: Or subverts a person’s lawsuit— does the Lord not see?
text.DRB: To pervert a man in his judgement, our Lord hath not known.

text.NRSV: Who can command and have it done, if the Lord has not ordained it?
text.Rotherham: Who was it that spake, and it was done, [when], My Lord, had not commanded?
text.KJV: Who is he that saith, and it cometh to pass, when the Lord commandeth it not?
text.NABRE: Who speaks so that it comes to pass, unless the Lord commands it?
text.DRB: Who is this, that hath commanded it to be done, our Lord not commanding it?

text.NRSV: Is it not from the mouth of the Most High that good and bad come?
text.Rotherham: Out of the mouth of the Most High, Proceed there not misfortunes and blessing?
text.KJV: Out of the mouth of the most High proceedeth not evil and good?
text.NABRE: Is it not at the word of the Most High that both good and bad take place?
text.DRB: Out of the mouth of the Highest, there shall not proceed neither evil things, nor good.

text.NRSV: Why should any who draw breath complain about the punishment of their sins?
text.Rotherham: Why should a living son of earth complain, [Let] a man [complain] because of his sins?
text.KJV: Wherefore doth a living man complain, a man for the punishment of his sins?
text.NABRE: What should the living complain about? about their sins!
text.DRB: What hath the living man murmured, man for his sins?

text.NRSV: Let us test and examine our ways, and return to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Let us search out our ways, and examine them well, and let us return unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the Lord.
text.NABRE: Let us search and examine our ways, and return to the Lord!
text.DRB: Let us search our ways, & seek, and return to our Lord.

text.NRSV: Let us lift up our hearts as well as our hands to God in heaven.
text.Rotherham: Let us lift up our heart, to the opened palms, to the Mighty One in the heavens;
text.KJV: Let us lift up our heart with our hands unto God in the heavens.
text.NABRE: Let us lift up our hearts as well as our hands toward God in heaven!
text.DRB: Let us lift up our hearts with our hands to our Lord into the heavens.

text.NRSV: We have transgressed and rebelled, and you have not forgiven.
text.Rotherham: We, have trespassed and rebelled, Thou, hast not pardoned.
text.KJV: We have transgressed and have rebelled: thou hast not pardoned.
text.NABRE: We have rebelled and been obstinate; you have not forgiven us.
text.DRB: We have done wickedly, and provoked to wrath: therefore thou art inexorable.

text.NRSV: You have wrapped yourself in anger and pursued us, killing without pity;
text.Rotherham: Thou hast covered thyself with anger, and pursued us, hast slain—hast not spared;
text.KJV: Thou hast covered with anger, and persecuted us: thou hast slain, thou hast not pitied.
text.NABRE: You wrapped yourself in wrath and pursued us, killing without pity;
text.DRB: Thou hast covered in fury, and hast strucken us: thou hast killed, and not spared.

text.NRSV: you have wrapped yourself in a cloud so that no prayer can pass through.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast screened thyself with the clouds, that prayer, should not pass through;
text.KJV: Thou hast covered thyself with a cloud, that our prayer should not pass through.
text.NABRE: You wrapped yourself in a cloud, which no prayer could pierce.
text.DRB: Thou hast set a cloud before thee, that prayer may not pass.

text.NRSV: You have made us filth and rubbish among the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Offscouring and refuse, dost thou make us, in the midst of the peoples.
text.KJV: Thou hast made us as the offscouring and refuse in the midst of the people.
text.NABRE: You have made us filth and rubbish among the peoples.
text.DRB: Thou hast made me to be rooted out, and abject in the midst of the peoples.

text.NRSV: All our enemies have opened their mouths against us;
text.Rotherham: With their mouth, opened wide over us, [stand] all our foes.
text.KJV: All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.
text.NABRE: They have opened their mouths against us, all our enemies;
text.DRB: All the enemies have opened their mouth upon us.

text.NRSV: panic and pitfall have come upon us, devastation and destruction.
text.Rotherham: Terror and a pit, have befallen us, tumult and grievous injury;
text.KJV: Fear and a snare is come upon us, desolation and destruction.
text.NABRE: Panic and the pit have been our lot, desolation and destruction;
text.DRB: Prophecy is made unto us, fear, and snare, and destruction.

text.NRSV: My eyes flow with rivers of tears because of the destruction of my people.
text.Rotherham: With streams of water, mine eye runneth down, over the grievous injury of the daughter of my people.
text.KJV: Mine eye runneth down with rivers of water for the destruction of the daughter of my people.
text.NABRE: My eyes stream with tears over the destruction of the daughter of my people.
text.DRB: Mine eye hath shed streams of waters, in the destruction of the daughter of my people.

text.NRSV: My eyes will flow without ceasing, without respite,
text.Rotherham: Mine eye, poureth itself out and ceaseth not, without relief;
text.KJV: Mine eye trickleth down, and ceaseth not, without any intermission,
text.NABRE: My eyes will flow without ceasing, without rest,
text.DRB: Mine eye is afflicted, neither hath it been quiet, because there was no rest:

text.NRSV: until the Lord from heaven looks down and sees.
text.Rotherham: Until Yahweh out of the heavens shall look forth, and see,
text.KJV: Till the Lord look down, and behold from heaven.
text.NABRE: Until the Lord from heaven looks down and sees.
text.DRB: Till our Lord regarded and looked from the heavens.

text.NRSV: My eyes cause me grief at the fate of all the young women in my city.
text.Rotherham: Mine eye dealeth severely with my soul, because of all the daughters of my city.
text.KJV: Mine eye affecteth mine heart because of all the daughters of my city.
text.NABRE: I am tormented by the sight of all the daughters of my city.
text.DRB: Mine eye hath spoiled my soul for all the daughters of my city.

text.NRSV: Those who were my enemies without cause have hunted me like a bird;
text.Rotherham: They, have laid snares, for me as a bird, who are mine enemies without cause:
text.KJV: Mine enemies chased me sore, like a bird, without cause.
text.NABRE: Without cause, my enemies snared me as though I were a bird;
text.DRB: Mine enemies in hunting have caught me as a bird, without cause.

text.NRSV: they flung me alive into a pit and hurled stones on me;
text.Rotherham: They have cut off, in the dungeon, my life, and have cast a stone upon me;
text.KJV: They have cut off my life in the dungeon, and cast a stone upon me.
text.NABRE: They tried to end my life in the pit, pelting me with stones.
text.DRB: My life is fallen into the lake, and they have laid a stone upon me.

text.NRSV: water closed over my head; I said, “I am lost.”
text.Rotherham: Waters, flowed over, my head, I said, I am cut off!
text.KJV: Waters flowed over mine head; then I said, I am cut off.
text.NABRE: The waters flowed over my head: and I said, “I am lost!”
text.DRB: The waters have flowed over my head: I said: I am undone.

text.NRSV: I called on your name, O Lord, from the depths of the pit;
text.Rotherham: I have called upon thy Name, O Yahweh, out of the dungeon below;
text.KJV: I called upon thy name, O Lord, out of the low dungeon.
text.NABRE: I have called upon your name, O Lord, from the bottom of the pit;
text.DRB: I have invocated thy name o Lord from the lowest lake.

text.NRSV: you heard my plea, “Do not close your ear to my cry for help, but give me relief!”
text.Rotherham: My voice, thou hast heard,—do not close thine ear to my respite, to mine outcry;
text.KJV: Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing, at my cry.
text.NABRE: You heard me call, “Do not let your ear be deaf to my cry for help.”
text.DRB: Thou hast heard my voice: turn not away thine ear from my sobbings, and cries.

text.NRSV: You came near when I called on you; you said, “Do not fear!”
text.Rotherham: Thou drewest near, in the day I kept calling on thee, thou saidst, Do not fear!
text.KJV: Thou drewest near in the day that I called upon thee: thou saidst, Fear not.
text.NABRE: You drew near on the day I called you; you said, “Do not fear!”
text.DRB: Thou didst approach in the day, when I invocated thee: thou hast said: Fear not.

text.NRSV: You have pleaded my cause, O Lord, you have redeemed my life.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast pleaded, O My Lord, the pleas of my soul, hast redeemed my life;
text.KJV: O Lord, thou hast pleaded the causes of my soul; thou hast redeemed my life.
text.NABRE: You pleaded my case, Lord, you redeemed my life.
text.DRB: Thou hast judged o Lord the cause of my soul, redeemer of my life.

text.NRSV: You have seen the wrong done to me, O Lord; judge my cause.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast beheld, O Yahweh, my failure to get justice, Pronounce thou my sentence;
text.KJV: O Lord, thou hast seen my wrong: judge thou my cause.
text.NABRE: You see, Lord, how I am wronged; do me justice!
text.DRB: Thou hast seen o Lord their iniquity against me: judge my judgement.

text.NRSV: You have seen all their malice, all their plots against me.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast seen all their vindictiveness, all their plots against me.
text.KJV: Thou hast seen all their vengeance and all their imaginations against me.
text.NABRE: You see all their vindictiveness, all their plots against me.
text.DRB: Thou hast seen all their fury, all their cogitations against me.

text.NRSV: You have heard their insults, O Lord, all their plots against me.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast heard their reproach, O Yahweh, all their plots against me;
text.KJV: Thou hast heard their reproach, O Lord, and all their imaginations against me;
text.NABRE: You hear their reproach, Lord, all their plots against me,
text.DRB: Thou hast heard their reproach o Lord, all their cogitations against me.

text.NRSV: The whispering and the murmuring of my assailants are against me all day long.
text.Rotherham: The lips of mine assailants, and their mutterings, are against me, all the day;
text.KJV: The lips of those that rose up against me, and their device against me all the day.
text.NABRE: The whispered murmurings of my adversaries, against me all day long;
text.DRB: The lips of them that rise up against me; and their cogitations against me all the day.

text.NRSV: Whether they sit or rise—see, I am the object of their taunt-songs.
text.Rotherham: On their downsitting and their uprising, do thou look, I, am their song.
text.KJV: Behold their sitting down, and their rising up; I am their musick.
text.NABRE: Look! Whether they sit or stand, I am the butt of their taunt.
text.DRB: See their sitting down, and their rising up, I am their psalm.

text.NRSV: Pay them back for their deeds, O Lord, according to the work of their hands!
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt render to them a recompense, O Yahweh, according to the work of their hands;
text.KJV: Render unto them a recompence, O Lord, according to the work of their hands.
text.NABRE: Give them what they deserve, Lord, according to their deeds;
text.DRB: Thou shalt render them a recompense o Lord, according to the works of their hands.

text.NRSV: Give them anguish of heart; your curse be on them!
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt suffer them a veiling of heart, thy curse to them;
text.KJV: Give them sorrow of heart, thy curse unto them.
text.NABRE: Give them hardness of heart; your curse be upon them;
text.DRB: Thou shalt give them a shield of heart thy labour.

text.NRSV: Pursue them in anger and destroy them from under the heavens of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt pursue in anger, and wilt destroy them, from under the heavens of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Persecute and destroy them in anger from under the heavens of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Pursue them in wrath and destroy them from under the Lord ’s heaven!
text.DRB: Thou shalt persecute in fury, and shalt destroy them from under the heavens o Lord.

text.NRSV: How the gold has grown dim, how the pure gold is changed! The holy stones lie scattered at the head of every street.
text.Rotherham: How is dimmed the gold! changed the most fine gold! Poured out are the stones of the sanctuary, at the top of all the streets.
text.KJV: How is the gold become dim! how is the most fine gold changed! the stones of the sanctuary are poured out in the top of every street.
text.NABRE: How the gold has lost its luster, the noble metal changed; Jewels lie scattered at the corner of every street.
text.DRB: How is the gold darkened, the best colour changed, the stones of the sanctuary dispersed in the head of all streets?

text.NRSV: The precious children of Zion, worth their weight in fine gold— how they are reckoned as earthen pots, the work of a potter’s hands!
text.Rotherham: The precious sons of Zion, they who were weighed against pure gold, How are they accounted as earthen pitchers, the work of the hands of the potter!
text.KJV: The precious sons of Zion, comparable to fine gold, how are they esteemed as earthen pitchers, the work of the hands of the potter!
text.NABRE: And Zion’s precious children, worth their weight in gold— How they are treated like clay jugs, the work of any potter!
text.DRB: The noble children of Sion, & they that were clothed with the principal gold: how are they reputed as earthen vessels, the work of the potter's hands?

text.NRSV: Even the jackals offer the breast and nurse their young, but my people has become cruel, like the ostriches in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Even wild dogs, draw out the breast, give suck to their whelps—The daughter of my people, hath become cruel, like the ostriches in the desert.
text.KJV: Even the sea monsters draw out the breast, they give suck to their young ones: the daughter of my people is become cruel, like the ostriches in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Even jackals offer their breasts to nurse their young; But the daughter of my people is as cruel as the ostrich in the wilderness.
text.DRB: Yea even the lamiæs have opened their breast, they have given suck to their young, the daughter of my people is cruel, as the ostrich in the desert.

text.NRSV: The tongue of the infant sticks to the roof of its mouth for thirst; the children beg for food, but no one gives them anything.
text.Rotherham: The tongue of the suckling, cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst,—Young children, have asked bread, there was none, to break, it to them.
text.KJV: The tongue of the sucking child cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst: the young children ask bread, and no man breaketh it unto them.
text.NABRE: The tongue of the infant cleaves to the roof of its mouth in thirst; Children beg for bread, but no one gives them a piece.
text.DRB: The tongue of the suckling hath cloven to the roof of his mouth for thirst: the little ones have asked bread, and there was none that brake it unto them.

text.NRSV: Those who feasted on delicacies perish in the streets; those who were brought up in purple cling to ash heaps.
text.Rotherham: They who used to eat delicacies, are deserted in the streets,—They who used to be carried on crimson, have embraced heaps of refuse.
text.KJV: They that did feed delicately are desolate in the streets: they that were brought up in scarlet embrace dunghills.
text.NABRE: Those who feasted on delicacies are abandoned in the streets; Those who reclined on crimson now embrace dung heaps.
text.DRB: They that fed voluptuously, have died in the ways: they that were brought up in scarlet, have embraced the dung.

text.NRSV: For the chastisement of my people has been greater than the punishment of Sodom, which was overthrown in a moment, though no hand was laid on it.
text.Rotherham: And the punishment of the daughter of my people hath grown greater than the punishment of Sodom,—which was overthrown as in a moment, when no hands had been laid violently upon her.
text.KJV: For the punishment of the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the punishment of the sin of Sodom, that was overthrown as in a moment, and no hands stayed on her.
text.NABRE: The punishment of the daughter of my people surpassed the penalty of Sodom, Which was overthrown in an instant with no hand laid on it.
text.DRB: And the iniquity of the daughter of my people is become greater than the sin of Sodom: which was overthrown in a moment, and hands took nothing in her.

text.NRSV: Her princes were purer than snow, whiter than milk; their bodies were more ruddy than coral, their hair like sapphire.
text.Rotherham: Purer were her Nazirites than snow, whiter were they than milk,—more ruddy, in body, than coral, A sapphire, was their beauty of form.
text.KJV: Her Nazarites were purer than snow, they were whiter than milk, they were more ruddy in body than rubies, their polishing was of sapphire:
text.NABRE: Her princes were brighter than snow, whiter than milk, Their bodies more ruddy than coral, their beauty like the sapphire.
text.DRB: Her Nazareites whiter than snow, purer than milk, ruddier than the old ivory, fairer than the sapphire.

text.NRSV: Now their appearance is blacker than soot; they are not recognized in the streets. Their skin has shriveled on their bones; it has become as dry as wood.
text.Rotherham: Darker than a coal, is their visage, They are not known in the streets—Their skin shrivelleth on their bones, is withered, become like a stick.
text.KJV: Their visage is blacker than a coal; they are not known in the streets: their skin cleaveth to their bones; it is withered, it is become like a stick.
text.NABRE: Now their appearance is blacker than soot, they go unrecognized in the streets; Their skin has shrunk on their bones, and become dry as wood.
text.DRB: Their face is made blacker than coals, and they are not known in the streets: their skin hath cloven to their bones, it is withered, and is made as wood.

text.NRSV: Happier were those pierced by the sword than those pierced by hunger, whose life drains away, unstabbed, for lack of the fruits of the field.
text.Rotherham: Better are the slain of the sword, than the slain of the famine,—for, these, pine away, stricken through, wanting the produce of the field.
text.KJV: They that be slain with the sword are better than they that be slain with hunger: for these pine away, stricken through for want of the fruits of the field.
text.NABRE: Better for those pierced by the sword than for those pierced by hunger, Better for those who bleed from wounds than for those who lack food.
text.DRB: It was better with them that were slain with the sword, than with them that were killed by famine: because these pined away consumed by the barrenness of the country.

text.NRSV: The hands of compassionate women have boiled their own children; they became their food in the destruction of my people.
text.Rotherham: The hands of compassionate women, have cooked their own children,—they have served as nourishment to them, in the grievous injury of the daughter of my people.
text.KJV: The hands of the pitiful women have sodden their own children: they were their meat in the destruction of the daughter of my people.
text.NABRE: The hands of compassionate women have boiled their own children! They became their food when the daughter of my people was shattered.
text.DRB: The hands of pitiful women have sodden their own children: they were made their meat in the destruction of the daughter of my people.

text.NRSV: The Lord gave full vent to his wrath; he poured out his hot anger, and kindled a fire in Zion that consumed its foundations.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh hath completed his indignation, hath poured out the glow of his anger; and hath kindled a fire in Zion, which hath devoured her foundations.
text.KJV: The Lord hath accomplished his fury; he hath poured out his fierce anger, and hath kindled a fire in Zion, and it hath devoured the foundations thereof.
text.NABRE: The Lord has exhausted his anger, poured out his blazing wrath; He has kindled a fire in Zion that has consumed her foundations.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath accomplished his fury, he hath poured out the wrath of his indignation: and he hath kindled a fire in Sion, and it hath devoured the foundations thereof.

text.NRSV: The kings of the earth did not believe, nor did any of the inhabitants of the world, that foe or enemy could enter the gates of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Neither the kings of the earth, nor any of the inhabitants of the world, believed, that an adversary or an enemy, should enter, the gates of Jerusalem!
