text.KJV: The kings of the earth, and all the inhabitants of the world, would not have believed that the adversary and the enemy should have entered into the gates of Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: The kings of the earth did not believe, nor any of the world’s inhabitants, That foe or enemy could enter the gates of Jerusalem.
text.DRB: The kings of the earth, and all the inhabitants of the world did not believe, that the adversary and the enemy should enter in by the gates of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: It was for the sins of her prophets and the iniquities of her priests, who shed the blood of the righteous in the midst of her.
text.Rotherham: [It is] for the sins of her prophets, the iniquities of her priests,—who have been pouring out, in her midst, the blood of the righteous!
text.KJV: For the sins of her prophets, and the iniquities of her priests, that have shed the blood of the just in the midst of her,
text.NABRE: Except for the sins of her prophets and the crimes of her priests, Who poured out in her midst the blood of the just.
text.DRB: For the sins of her prophets, and the iniquities of her priests, which have shed the blood of just men in the midst of her.

text.NRSV: Blindly they wandered through the streets, so defiled with blood that no one was able to touch their garments.
text.Rotherham: They have wandered—blind—in the streets, have defiled themselves with blood; so that men may not touch their garments.
text.KJV: They have wandered as blind men in the streets, they have polluted themselves with blood, so that men could not touch their garments.
text.NABRE: They staggered blindly in the streets, defiled with blood, So that people could not touch even their garments:
text.DRB: The blind wandered in the streets, they were polluted with blood: and when they could not, they held their skirts.

text.NRSV: “Away! Unclean!” people shouted at them; “Away! Away! Do not touch!” So they became fugitives and wanderers; it was said among the nations, “They shall stay here no longer.”
text.Rotherham: Turn aside! Unclean! have they cried to them, Turn aside! Turn aside! Do not touch! because they have fled, yea, wandered, Men said among the nations, They will not again tarry;
text.KJV: They cried unto them, Depart ye; it is unclean; depart, depart, touch not: when they fled away and wandered, they said among the heathen, They shall no more sojourn there.
text.NABRE: “Go away! Unclean!” they cried to them, “Away, away, do not touch!” If they went away and wandered, it would be said among the nations, “They can no longer live here!
text.DRB: Depart ye polluted, they cried to them: depart, get ye hence, touch not: for they brawled, & were moved: they said among the Gentiles: He will add no more to dwell among them.

text.NRSV: The Lord himself has scattered them, he will regard them no more; no honor was shown to the priests, no favor to the elders.
text.Rotherham: The face of Yahweh, hath scattered them, He will look them out no more: The persons of the priests, they have not respected, To the elders, have they shown no favour.
text.KJV: The anger of the Lord hath divided them; he will no more regard them: they respected not the persons of the priests, they favoured not the elders.
text.NABRE: The presence of the Lord was their portion, but he no longer looks upon them. The priests are shown no regard, the elders, no mercy.
text.DRB: The face of our Lord hath divided them, he will not add to respect them: they have not reverenced the faces of the priests, neither had they pity on the ancients.

text.NRSV: Our eyes failed, ever watching vainly for help; we were watching eagerly for a nation that could not save.
text.Rotherham: Silly shall our eyes fail, for our help that is vain: In our watchtower, have we watched—for a nation that will not save.
text.KJV: As for us, our eyes as yet failed for our vain help: in our watching we have watched for a nation that could not save us.
text.NABRE: Even now our eyes are worn out, searching in vain for help; From our watchtower we have watched for a nation unable to save.
text.DRB: Whilst we yet stood, our eyes failed towards our vain help, when we looked attentive to a nation, that was not able to save.

text.NRSV: They dogged our steps so that we could not walk in our streets; our end drew near; our days were numbered; for our end had come.
text.Rotherham: They have laid snares for our steps, that we cannot walk in our own broadways: Drawn near hath our end, Fulfilled are our days, yea arrived hath our end.
text.KJV: They hunt our steps, that we cannot go in our streets: our end is near, our days are fulfilled; for our end is come.
text.NABRE: They dogged our every step, we could not walk in our squares; Our end drew near, our time was up; yes, our end had come.
text.DRB: Our steps slipped in the way of our streets, our end draweth near: our days are accomplished, because our end is come.

text.NRSV: Our pursuers were swifter than the eagles in the heavens; they chased us on the mountains, they lay in wait for us in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Swifter are our pursuers, than the eagles of the heavens,—Over the mountains, have they come hotly after us, In the wilderness, have they lain in wait for us.
text.KJV: Our persecutors are swifter than the eagles of the heaven: they pursued us upon the mountains, they laid wait for us in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Our pursuers were swifter than eagles in the sky, In the mountains they were hot on our trail, they ambushed us in the wilderness.
text.DRB: Our persecuters were swifter than the eagles of the heaven: upon the mountains they pursued us, in the desert they lay in wait against us.

text.NRSV: The Lord’s anointed, the very breath of our life, was caught in their pits— the one of whom we said, “Under his shadow we shall live among the nations.”
text.Rotherham: The fragrance of our nostrils, The Anointed of Yahweh, hath been captured in their pits,—of whom we had said—In his shade, shall we live among the nations.
text.KJV: The breath of our nostrils, the anointed of the Lord, was taken in their pits, of whom we said, Under his shadow we shall live among the heathen.
text.NABRE: The Lord ’s anointed—our very lifebreath!— was caught in their snares, He in whose shade we thought to live among the nations.
text.DRB: The spirit of our mouth Christ our Lord is taken in our sins: to whom we have said: In thy shadow shall we live among the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: Rejoice and be glad, O daughter Edom, you who live in the land of Uz; but to you also the cup shall pass; you shall become drunk and strip yourself bare.
text.Rotherham: Rejoice and be glad, O daughter of Edom, thou inhabitress in the and of Uz,—Even unto thee, shall the cup pass along, thou shalt be drunken, and expose thy shame.
text.KJV: Rejoice and be glad, O daughter of Edom, that dwellest in the land of Uz; the cup also shall pass through unto thee: thou shalt be drunken, and shalt make thyself naked.
text.NABRE: Rejoice and gloat, daughter Edom, dwelling in the land of Uz, The cup will pass to you as well; you shall become drunk and strip yourself naked!
text.DRB: Rejoice, and be glad o daughter of Edom, which dwellest in the Land of Hus: to thee also shall the cup come, thou shalt be made drunken, and naked.

text.NRSV: The punishment of your iniquity, O daughter Zion, is accomplished, he will keep you in exile no longer; but your iniquity, O daughter Edom, he will punish, he will expose your sins.
text.Rotherham: Completed is thy punishment, O daughter of Zion, He will no more carry thee away captive—He hath punished thine iniquity, O daughter of Edom, he hath stripped the veil from off thy sins.
text.KJV: The punishment of thine iniquity is accomplished, O daughter of Zion; he will no more carry thee away into captivity: he will visit thine iniquity, O daughter of Edom; he will discover thy sins.
text.NABRE: Your punishment is completed, daughter Zion, the Lord will not prolong your exile; The Lord will punish your iniquity, daughter Edom, will lay bare your sins.
text.DRB: Thine iniquity is accomplished o daughter of Sion, he will add no more to transport thee: he hath visited thine iniquity o daughter of Edom, he hath discovered thy sins.

text.NRSV: Remember, O Lord, what has befallen us; look, and see our disgrace!
text.Rotherham: Remember, O Yahweh, what hath befallen us, Look around, and see our reproach:
text.KJV: Remember, O Lord, what is come upon us: consider, and behold our reproach.
text.NABRE: Remember, Lord, what has happened to us, pay attention, and see our disgrace:
text.DRB: Remember o Lord what is fallen to us: behold, and regard our reproach.

text.NRSV: Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers, our homes to aliens.
text.Rotherham: Our inheritance, turned over to foreigners, our houses, to aliens.
text.KJV: Our inheritance is turned to strangers, our houses to aliens.
text.NABRE: Our heritage is turned over to strangers, our homes, to foreigners.
text.DRB: Our inheritance is turned to aliens: our houses to strangers.

text.NRSV: We have become orphans, fatherless; our mothers are like widows.
text.Rotherham: Orphans, have we become, and fatherless, our mothers, are widows indeed.
text.KJV: We are orphans and fatherless, our mothers are as widows.
text.NABRE: We have become orphans, without fathers; our mothers are like widows.
text.DRB: We are made pupils without father: our mothers are as it were widows.

text.NRSV: We must pay for the water we drink; the wood we get must be bought.
text.Rotherham: Our water—for silver, have we drunk, our wood—for a price, cometh in.
text.KJV: We have drunken our water for money; our wood is sold unto us.
text.NABRE: We pay money to drink our own water, our own wood comes at a price.
text.DRB: Our water we have drunk for money: our wood we have bought for a price.

text.NRSV: With a yoke on our necks we are hard driven; we are weary, we are given no rest.
text.Rotherham: Upon our necks, are our pursuers, We labour, and there is allowed us no rest.
text.KJV: Our necks are under persecution: we labour, and have no rest.
text.NABRE: With a yoke on our necks, we are driven; we are worn out, but allowed no rest.
text.DRB: We were led by our necks, no rest was given to the weary.

text.NRSV: We have made a pact with Egypt and Assyria, to get enough bread.
text.Rotherham: To Egypt, have we stretched out our hand, to Assyria, to be satisfied with bread.
text.KJV: We have given the hand to the Egyptians, and to the Assyrians, to be satisfied with bread.
text.NABRE: We extended a hand to Egypt and Assyria, to satisfy our need of bread.
text.DRB: We have given our hand to Ægypt, and to the Assyrians, that we might be filled with bread.

text.NRSV: Our ancestors sinned; they are no more, and we bear their iniquities.
text.Rotherham: Our fathers, sinned, and are not, and, we, their iniquities, have borne.
text.KJV: Our fathers have sinned, and are not; and we have borne their iniquities.
text.NABRE: Our ancestors, who sinned, are no more; but now we bear their guilt.
text.DRB: Our fathers have sinned, & they are not: and we have borne their iniquities.

text.NRSV: Slaves rule over us, and there is no one to deliver us from their hand.
text.Rotherham: Slaves, have ruled over us, There is none to set free from their hand.
text.KJV: Servants have ruled over us: there is none that doth deliver us out of their hand.
text.NABRE: Servants rule over us, with no one to tear us from their hands.
text.DRB: Servants have ruled over us: there was none that would redeem us out of their hand.

text.NRSV: We get our bread at the peril of our lives, because of the sword in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: At the risk of our life, do we bring in our bread, because of the sword of the desert.
text.KJV: We gat our bread with the peril of our lives because of the sword of the wilderness.
text.NABRE: We risk our lives just to get bread, exposed to the desert heat;
text.DRB: In peril of our lives did we fetch us bread, at the face of the sword in the desert.

text.NRSV: Our skin is black as an oven from the scorching heat of famine.
text.Rotherham: Our skin, as with a furnace, is scorched, because of the hot winds of famine.
text.KJV: Our skin was black like an oven because of the terrible famine.
text.NABRE: Our skin heats up like an oven, from the searing blasts of famine.
text.DRB: Our skin was burnt as an oven, by reason of the tempests of famine.

text.NRSV: Women are raped in Zion, virgins in the towns of Judah.
text.Rotherham: Women—in Zion, were ravished, virgins, in the cities of Judah!
text.KJV: They ravished the women in Zion, and the maids in the cities of Judah.
text.NABRE: Women are raped in Zion, young women in the cities of Judah;
text.DRB: They humbled the women in Sion, and the virgins in the cities of Juda.

text.NRSV: Princes are hung up by their hands; no respect is shown to the elders.
text.Rotherham: Princes, by their hand, have been hanged, The faces of elders, not honoured.
text.KJV: Princes are hanged up by their hand: the faces of elders were not honoured.
text.NABRE: Princes have been hanged by them, elders shown no respect.
text.DRB: The Princes were hanged up by the hand: they did not reverence the faces of the ancients.

text.NRSV: Young men are made to grind at the mill, and boys stagger under loads of wood.
text.Rotherham: Young men, a millstone, have lifted, and, youths, under wood, have staggered.
text.KJV: They took the young men to grind, and the children fell under the wood.
text.NABRE: Young men carry millstones, boys stagger under loads of wood;
text.DRB: Young men they abused unchastely: and the children fell in wood.

text.NRSV: The old men have left the city gate, the young men their music.
text.Rotherham: Elders, from the gates, have ceased. Young men, from their music.
text.KJV: The elders have ceased from the gate, the young men from their musick.
text.NABRE: The elders have abandoned the gate, the young men their music.
text.DRB: The ancients decayed out of the gates: the youngmen out of the choir of the singers.

text.NRSV: The joy of our hearts has ceased; our dancing has been turned to mourning.
text.Rotherham: Ceased hath the joy of our hearts, Changed to mourning, our dance.
text.KJV: The joy of our heart is ceased; our dance is turned into mourning.
text.NABRE: The joy of our hearts has ceased, dancing has turned into mourning;
text.DRB: The joy of our heart hath failed, our choir is turned into mourning.

text.NRSV: The crown has fallen from our head; woe to us, for we have sinned!
text.Rotherham: Fallen is the crown of our head. Surely woe to us, for we have sinned.
text.KJV: The crown is fallen from our head: woe unto us, that we have sinned!
text.NABRE: The crown has fallen from our head: woe to us that we sinned!
text.DRB: The crown of our head is fallen: woe to us, because we have sinned.

text.NRSV: Because of this our hearts are sick, because of these things our eyes have grown dim:
text.Rotherham: For this cause, faint is our heart, For these things, dimmed are our eyes:
text.KJV: For this our heart is faint; for these things our eyes are dim.
text.NABRE: Because of this our hearts grow sick, at this our eyes grow dim:
text.DRB: Therefore is our heart made sorrowful, therefore are our eyes darkened.

text.NRSV: because of Mount Zion, which lies desolate; jackals prowl over it.
text.Rotherham: Because of Mount Zion, which is desolate, jackals, have gone prowling therein.
text.KJV: Because of the mountain of Zion, which is desolate, the foxes walk upon it.
text.NABRE: Because of Mount Zion, lying desolate, and the jackals roaming there!
text.DRB: For mount Sion, because it is perished, foxes have walked on it.

text.NRSV: But you, O Lord, reign forever; your throne endures to all generations.
text.Rotherham: Thou, O Yahweh, unto times age-abiding, dost remain, Thy throne, from generation to generation:
text.KJV: Thou, O Lord, remainest for ever; thy throne from generation to generation.
text.NABRE: But you, Lord, are enthroned forever; your throne stands from age to age.
text.DRB: But thou o Lord shalt remain for ever, thy throne in generation and to generation.

text.NRSV: Why have you forgotten us completely? Why have you forsaken us these many days?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore shouldst thou perpetually forget us? forsake us, to length of days?
text.KJV: Wherefore dost thou forget us for ever, and forsake us so long time?
text.NABRE: Why have you utterly forgotten us, forsaken us for so long?
text.DRB: Why wilt thou for ever be forgetful of us? Wilt thou forsake us in length of days?

text.NRSV: Restore us to yourself, O Lord, that we may be restored; renew our days as of old—
text.Rotherham: Bring us back, O Yahweh, unto thyself, and we will come back! Renew our days, as of old;
text.KJV: Turn thou us unto thee, O Lord, and we shall be turned; renew our days as of old.
text.NABRE: Bring us back to you, Lord, that we may return: renew our days as of old.
text.DRB: Convert us o Lord to thee, and we shall be converted: renew our days, as from the beginning.

text.NRSV: unless you have utterly rejected us, and are angry with us beyond measure.
text.Rotherham: For though thou hast not, utterly rejected, us, thou art wroth with us—exceedingly!
text.KJV: But thou hast utterly rejected us; thou art very wroth against us.
text.NABRE: For now you have indeed rejected us and utterly turned your wrath against us.
text.DRB: But rejecting thou hast rejected us, thou art angry against us exceedingly.
